name: remote-terminal-image-paste
description: Paste Windows clipboard images into Remote-SSH terminals by writing them to the remote workspace and inserting the image path.

commands.title: Remote Terminal Image Paste: Paste Image To Remote Terminal
Remote Terminal Image Paste: Cleanup Saved Images

remoteTerminalImagePaste.shortcut.markdownDescription: Shortcut used to trigger image paste in the terminal. The default is `Ctrl+Alt+V`. Select `none` to disable the contributed shortcut and use the command palette only.

remoteTerminalImagePaste.remoteDirectory.markdownDescription: Directory used on the remote machine. Relative paths resolve under the first workspace folder. Absolute paths are used as-is on the remote machine.

remoteTerminalImagePaste.fileNameTemplat.markdownDescription: Remote file name template. Supported tokens: `{timestamp}`, `{date}`, `{time}`, `{random}`.

remoteTerminalImagePaste.insertTemplate.markdownDescription: Template inserted into the terminal after the image is materialized. Supported tokens: `{path}`, `{quotedPath}`, `{workspaceRelativePath}`, `{fileName}`.

remoteTerminalImagePaste.autoAddGitIgnor.markdownDescription: Automatically ensure the remote image directory is ignored by the workspace `.gitignore`.

remoteTerminalImagePaste.maxFileSizeMb.markdownDescription: Maximum clipboard image size in MB before the paste is blocked.

remoteTerminalImagePaste.cleanup.maxFile.markdownDescription: Keep at most this many newest pasted images in the remote directory. Set to `0` to disable count-based cleanup.

remoteTerminalImagePaste.cleanup.maxAgeD.markdownDescription: Delete pasted images older than this many days. Set to `0` to disable age-based cleanup.

remoteTerminalImagePaste.confirmBeforeUp.markdownDescription: Show a confirmation prompt before creating a remote file.

remoteTerminalImagePaste.outputChannelVe.markdownDescription: Emit detailed logs to the output channel.
